bug_report.summary: URL bar one-off/search-provider icon changes unexpectedly on youtube.com (generic icon replaced by YouTube favicon/icon)
steps_to_reproduce: Navigate to https://www.youtube.com/ (as in the scenario).
Click the address bar to open the autocomplete panel (showing “This time, search with:” one-off icons).
expected_behaviors: The one-off/search-provider icons row should be consistent across builds for the same page/state (no unexpected replacement of a provider icon).
actual_behaviors: A one-off/search-provider icon at the same position in the row changes between builds (before: generic magnifier/add-search style icon; after: red YouTube/favicon-like icon).

bug_report.summary: Back from DuckDuckGo Images tab does not restore persisted search terms for non-default engine search
steps_to_reproduce: Set Default Search Engine to Bing (so DuckDuckGo is non-default).
In a new tab, open the address bar, use DuckDuckGo one-off, search for "firefox containers".
On the DuckDuckGo SERP, click the “Images” tab.
Click the browser Back button to return to the original results page.
expected_behaviors: After returning via Back, the address bar should again display the persisted search terms (e.g., “firefox containers”) and show the persisted-search UI (chip/chiclet) on the original SERP.
actual_behaviors: After returning via Back, the address bar continues to show the full DuckDuckGo URL (e.g., duckduckgo.com/...&ia=web) with normal site identity/star icons; persisted search terms UI is not restored.

bug_report.summary: Address bar right-side icon changes from site identity (globe) to 'Go/Search' arrow after switching search mode (Google one-off)
steps_to_reproduce: Enable persisted search terms prefs (browser.urlbar.showSearchTerms.featureGate=true and browser.urlbar.showSearchTerms.enabled=true).
Set default search engine to Bing so DuckDuckGo is non-default.
Perform a search using DuckDuckGo one-off (e.g., 'site privacy settings') to load a DuckDuckGo SERP with persisted-search terms showing in the urlbar.
Click the address bar to open the search panel/one-offs.
Click the Google one-off button to switch search mode.
expected_behaviors: Switching search mode should not change the urlbar’s right-side page/identity indicator unexpectedly; the urlbar should remain in a consistent state after the mode switch (e.g., showing the normal page identity icon when not actively editing/typing).
actual_behaviors: After the mode switch, the icon at the right side of the urlbar appears as a 'Go/Search' arrow instead of the normal site identity (globe) icon (difference visible at the right end of the address bar).

bug_report.summary: Urlbar autocomplete panel shows large unexpected empty space below one-off search engines row
steps_to_reproduce: Perform a Google search (default engine) and land on the SERP.
Click the address bar to open the urlbar autocomplete panel (showing suggestions and one-off engines).
expected_behaviors: The urlbar autocomplete panel height should tightly fit its contents (suggestions + one-off engines row), with only normal padding at the bottom.
actual_behaviors: The urlbar panel extends noticeably below the one-off engines row, leaving a large blank/empty area at the bottom of the dropdown and overlapping more of the page than necessary.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering
